phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q-channel last hits only — you are the weakest level-1 champion in the lane. Give ground, take CS, stack the first stardust.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Anivia's wall-frost poke control own this window — your stardust ramp control don't answer them at level 2 yet. Farm what's safe and let the next spike re-price the lane.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: At level 3 the numbers lean his way — the wall-frost poke control out-tick your stardust ramp control in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: Slight deficit through levels 4-5: Anivia's wall-frost poke control edge out your stardust ramp control on the margins. You can still trade — just let him start the exchanges and answer on your terms.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint the numbers lean his way — the wall-frost poke control out-tick your stardust ramp control in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: RoA/Seraph timing: your E executes the back line of every wave and the stardust curve bends upward for good.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: You are the scaling endgame: The Skies Descend flips entire objective fights and your Q melts anything that stands in the singularity.

phases.label: Level 1
phases.why: Q-channel last hits only — you are the weakest level-1 champion in the lane. Give ground, take CS, stack the first stardust.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Still farming. Drag the Q beam across his face only when he steps onto your caster minions — every champion tick is permanent stacks.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E online: drop the black hole ON the wave and Q the pull — your first real clear pattern, and every execute tick stacks stardust.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: W lets you Q while flying — the burn-flyby trade is real now. In-and-out: never land inside his engage range.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Falling Star online — the self-peel stun your kit was missing. With R up, his dive math suddenly has a second variable.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Still farming. Drag the Q beam across his face only when he steps onto your caster minions — every champion tick is permanent stacks.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E online: drop the black hole ON the wave and Q the pull — your first real clear pattern, and every execute tick stacks stardust.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: W lets you Q while flying — the burn-flyby trade is real now. In-and-out: never land inside his engage range.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: At the level-6 breakpoint the numbers lean his way — the reflect-barrier poke out-tick your stardust ramp control in even exchanges. Don't bleed for pride; take the trades his cooldowns hand you.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: Slight edge on first item: your stardust ramp control beat Mel's reflect-barrier poke on the margins. Take the short trades you start — just don't force a coin-flip all-in off a small lead.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: At two-plus items the numbers lean your way — stardust ramp control edge out the reflect-barrier poke when both sides play it straight. Press the small advantages: wave position, first poke, cooldown ticks.

phases.why: Falling Star online — the self-peel stun your kit was missing. With R up, his dive math suddenly has a second variable.

phases.why: At two-plus items your stardust ramp control beat the clone-root deception in a straight exchange. Play the front foot — make Neeko answer you, not the other way around.

phases.why: Q-channel last hits only — you are the weakest level-1 champion in the lane. Give ground, take CS, stack the first stardust.

phases.label: Level 2
phases.why: Still farming. Drag the Q beam across his face only when he steps onto your caster minions — every champion tick is permanent stacks.

phases.label: Level 3
phases.why: E online: drop the black hole ON the wave and Q the pull — your first real clear pattern, and every execute tick stacks stardust.

phases.label: Levels 4-5
phases.why: W lets you Q while flying — the burn-flyby trade is real now. In-and-out: never land inside his engage range.

phases.label: Level 6
phases.why: Falling Star online — the self-peel stun your kit was missing. With R up, his dive math suddenly has a second variable.

phases.label: First item
phases.why: RoA/Seraph timing: your E executes the back line of every wave and the stardust curve bends upward for good.

phases.label: 2+ items
phases.why: You are the scaling endgame: The Skies Descend flips entire objective fights and your Q melts anything that stands in the singularity.
